header.name: Guru Subramani PhD
header.title: Enabling robots to touch and feel the world.
header.bio: My work focuses on imitation learning for physical AI.

sections.title: Areas of Research
sections.description: Forces and moments in human demonstrations reveal tactile manipulations and constraint interactions. Converting these observations into semantic understanding enables robots to robustly interact with their environment.

items.label: Imitation Learning
items.description: Learning policies from human demonstrations and teleoperation.

items.label: Modeling Physical Interaction
items.description: Modeling contact-rich interactions and their semantics.

items.label: Physical AI
items.description: Feature representations for tactile and proprioceptive sensing.

items.label: Robot Control Systems
items.description: Designing controllers that connect perception to actuation.

items.title: Recognizing Actions during Tactile Manipulations through Force Sensing
items.description: A method for identifying and temporally localizing tactile force actions using the Continuous Wavelet Transform with Complex Morlet wavelets. Demonstrated successful recognition of various manipulation actions from force signals.

items.title: Constraint Inference from Human Demonstrations
items.description: A method for inferring geometric constraints such as hinges, drawers, and planar contact directly from human demonstrations using pose, force, and moment measurements. The system identifies both the constraint type and its geometric parameters without assuming contact geometry in advance.

items.label: Reinforcement Learning for Robotics
items.description: The exploration exploitation trade-off is what keeps reinforcement learning from solving robotics. Simulations help but the fidelity gap and compute constraints remain a challenge.

items.title: A method for constraint inference using pose and wrench measurements
